mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into positive corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series reverts to its 12-month mean within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in the index of industrial production reflect accelerating manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes falls below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation remains elevated above 3% annually while the federal funds rate stays low, markets price in aggressive future tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 70% of observations when both conditions are met simultaneously over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter changes show zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic momentum and wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 20d returns in > 60% of months following payrolls beats above the 200k threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a synchronized demand-supply contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The dual decline signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns when the Federal Reserve simultaneously announces emergency rate cuts.
